domain: Old Norse skaldic kenning (Scandinavian court poetry; 2-noun circumlocution referring to unspoken 3rd noun; recursively nested e.g. 'horse of the swan's road' = ship; tvíkent + nýgerving allow 3-4 levels deep; decoder must know mythology to resolve)
specific_mechanism: Recursive substitution of 2-noun kennings — each substitution compresses (2,2) → 1 referent; chain depth 2-4 typical; referent preserved across levels; cultural mythology serves as decode key.
llm_application: KENNING-CASCADE: a context-compression cascade with N recursive stages. Stage k takes 2^(N-k+1) anchor tokens and fuses them pairwise into 2^(N-k) anchor tokens using a SHARED FUSION FUNCTION f(a_i, a_j) → b. f is implemented as a small cross-attention block: b = CrossAttn(query=fused_pair, keys=values=original_context). After N stages, only 2^0=1 super-anchor remains (down from 2^N), but the full context is REFERENT-RECOVERABLE because each stage's cross-attention keys point back to original tokens. Decoding LLM uses pretrained background knowledge (the 'mythology library') to interpret the super-anchor.
novelty_claim: AdmTree gist-token hierarchical summarization uses ONE-LEVEL gist tokens summarising independent blocks. CCF segment-wise aggregation is single-pass. Autoencoding-Free Contextual Semantic Anchors is single-stage. KENNING-CASCADE differs by (a) RECURSIVE MULTIPLICATIVE 2-to-1 fusion, (b) LOG-DEPTH cascade producing factor 2^N compression, (c) REFERENT-PRESERVATION via every-stage cross-attention back to original tokens (not via reconstruction loss), (d) decoder explicitly RELIES on pretrained background as 'mythology library'.
